title: Rail Artifact Identity Contract
description: Example single-document Stenc spec for Rail artifact identity and projection authority.

page.humanSummary: Rail work is identified by artifact handles, not request files or inferred task names. Users should be able to resume or inspect work through durable artifacts.
page.agentSummary: When changing artifact identity, update runtime contracts, tests, and any skill surface that describes start, resume, retry, status, or result behavior.

body.goal: Show how one durable runtime contract can name identity rules, owned surfaces, validation, and agent instructions in one JSON document.
body.problem: Artifact identity rules are easy to blur when plans mention request files, target-local state, or generated output. That can make agents infer the wrong resume or projection authority.

architecture.summary: Rail separates request drafting, artifact persistence, supervision, and result projection. Artifact identity belongs to the persisted artifact layer, while supervision and result projection consume validated handles.

requirements.title: Durable artifact identity
requirements.detail: Rail work must be identified by a persisted artifact handle rather than by request file paths or inferred task names.
acceptanceCriteria: Fresh work creates a fresh artifact handle.
Resume and retry flows require a validated existing handle.

approaches.name: Artifact-handle contract
tradeoffs: Prevents agents from guessing resume authority from nearby files
approaches.recommendation: Keep artifact handles as the only resume and result-projection authority.

components.name: Artifact persistence
components.responsibility: Own durable handles and persisted run artifacts.

errorHandling.behavior: Stop resume or retry work and report the missing validated handle instead of inferring identity.

contracts.name: Artifact identity
rules: Fresh work creates a fresh artifact handle.
Existing work resumes only from a validated handle.

testingStrategy.expected: Artifact identity, resume, retry, and projection tests pass.

testingStrategy.command: uv run --python 3.12 ruff check src tests

validation.command: uv run --python 3.12 ruff check src tests

agentInstructions: Read this spec before changing artifact identity.
Update linked skills and tests when public artifact behavior changes.
reviewChecklist: The source of truth is explicit and current.
Contract rules are testable or tied to validation evidence.

selfReviewChecks.name: Completeness
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Confirm identity, resume, retry, and result projection are all represented.

selfReviewChecks.name: Ambiguity check
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Confirm no text allows agents to infer identity from request files.

notes: Create a task-by-task implementation plan before changing runtime behavior.

supportingSections.heading: Reviewer Calibration
supportingSections.content: Only flag identity wording that could change runtime behavior or confuse artifact ownership.

links.label: Architecture
links.purpose: Explains artifact persistence and result projection boundaries.

steps.title: Check resume authority
steps.instruction: Confirm resume and retry wording names artifact handles, not request files.

subSections.heading: Nested Review Detail
subSections.content: Use nested sections for legacy outline detail that has no native Stenc field.
items: Keep canonical rules in body.contracts first.
